angry.description: A high-intensity emotional state driven by frustration or aggression, best matched by music with raw power and confrontational energy.
angry.critique_guidance: Check that recommended songs have high energy (0.75+) and low valence (below 0.4); mellow or happy-sounding tracks are a mismatch for angry mood.

chill.description: A laid-back, easygoing state that calls for relaxed and unhurried music without demanding attention.
chill.critique_guidance: Verify that songs are low-to-mid energy (below 0.5) and carry a mildly positive valence; overly intense or sad tracks undermine the chill atmosphere.

confident.description: A self-assured, empowered state that pairs well with music that feels bold, polished, and assertive.
confident.critique_guidance: Ensure tracks carry moderate-to-high energy and a clearly positive valence; low-energy or melancholic songs conflict with the confident mood profile.

euphoric.description: An intensely joyful, almost overwhelming state of elation best supported by high-energy, bright-sounding music.
euphoric.critique_guidance: Both energy and valence should be very high (0.8+); anything low-energy or emotionally ambiguous is a poor fit for euphoric mood.

focused.description: A state of deep concentration that benefits from music that is steady and unobtrusive without being distracting.
focused.critique_guidance: Look for low-to-mid energy tracks with neutral or slightly positive valence; high-energy or emotionally charged songs will break concentration.

happy.description: A bright, cheerful emotional state that is best complemented by upbeat, feel-good music with positive energy.
happy.critique_guidance: Songs should have clearly positive valence (0.7+) and moderate-to-high energy; dark or slow tracks are a mismatch for happy mood.

intense.description: A state of heightened focus and emotional pressure that demands music with high energy and dramatic impact.
intense.critique_guidance: Energy must be very high (0.75+); valence can range widely but tracks that feel passive or quiet contradict the intense mood profile.

melancholic.description: A reflective, sorrowful state that resonates with music expressing sadness, longing, or quiet introspection.
melancholic.critique_guidance: Both energy and valence should be low; upbeat or high-energy tracks feel jarring and inappropriate for a melancholic mood.

moody.description: An emotionally complex, brooding state that suits music with shifting dynamics and an introspective quality.
moody.critique_guidance: Mid-range energy and below-average valence are the target; overly cheerful or extremely aggressive tracks both miss the nuanced moody profile.

nostalgic.description: A bittersweet longing for the past that is well served by warm, familiar-sounding music with emotional depth.
nostalgic.critique_guidance: Tracks should sit in a moderate energy and mid-positive valence range; overly aggressive or clinically modern production undercuts the nostalgic feel.

peaceful.description: A calm, serene state of inner stillness that pairs naturally with gentle, unhurried music that soothes rather than stimulates.
peaceful.critique_guidance: Energy must stay low (below 0.4) while valence should be gently positive; anything loud, harsh, or emotionally heavy disrupts the peaceful mood.

relaxed.description: A comfortable, at-ease state that benefits from music that is smooth and flowing without demanding active listening.
relaxed.critique_guidance: Songs should be low energy and carry a neutral-to-positive valence; high-energy or emotionally intense tracks conflict with a relaxed listening state.

romantic.description: An emotionally intimate, tender state that calls for music evoking warmth, closeness, and heartfelt feeling.
romantic.critique_guidance: Tracks should feel warm and emotionally expressive with moderate-positive valence; cold electronic textures or aggressive energy undermine the romantic mood.

uplifting.description: An inspiring, motivating state that is amplified by music that feels hopeful, energetic, and emotionally expansive.
uplifting.critique_guidance: Both energy and valence should be above average; low-energy or emotionally negative tracks directly oppose the uplifting character of this mood.
